terms.definition: Free - getting something without paying any money. The word 'muft' makes everyone excited! In the story, people go crazy when they hear things are muft. But the story teaches that nothing in life is truly free - there is always some cost or catch.
terms.exampleUsage: बच्चों को मुफ्त में किताबें बाँटी गईं। (Books were distributed to children for free.)

terms.definition: Greed - wanting more and more of something, especially more than you need. In the story, the lalach of getting free things makes people behave in funny ways. The Hindi proverb says 'लालच बुरी बला' meaning greed is an evil thing.
terms.exampleUsage: लालच में आकर उसने सारा सामान उठा लिया। (Driven by greed, he picked up all the stuff.)

terms.definition: Humour or comedy - something that makes you laugh. This story is full of hasya because the way people behave when they hear 'free' is very funny. Hasya is an important part of Hindi literature and makes stories enjoyable to read.
terms.exampleUsage: यह कहानी हास्य से भरी है। (This story is full of humour.)

terms.definition: Contentment or satisfaction - being happy with what you have. Santosh is the opposite of lalach (greed). The story teaches that having santosh is important - we should be grateful for what we have instead of always wanting more free things.
terms.exampleUsage: संतोष सबसे बड़ा धन है। (Contentment is the greatest wealth.)

terms.definition: Value or price - how much something is worth. Every thing has a mulya, whether it is money or effort. The story teaches that even 'free' things have some mulya - someone has paid for them or there is a hidden condition. We should understand the real mulya of things.
terms.exampleUsage: हर चीज़ का कोई न कोई मूल्य होता है। (Everything has some value.)

terms.definition: To collect or gather hastily - picking up many things quickly. In the story, people start batorana (grabbing and collecting) free things as fast as they can. It shows how greed makes people grab everything without thinking.
terms.exampleUsage: उसने मुफ्त का सामान बटोर लिया। (He grabbed the free stuff quickly.)

terms.definition: Excitement or enthusiasm - a strong feeling of interest and eagerness. When people hear that something is free, they feel great utsaah (excitement). But too much utsaah without thinking can lead to trouble, as the story shows.
terms.exampleUsage: 'मुफ्त' सुनकर सबमें उत्साह आ गया। (Hearing 'free', everyone became excited.)

terms.definition: Self-control or restraint - the ability to control your desires and behaviour. Sanyam means not grabbing everything just because it is free. The story teaches that having sanyam is important - think first, then act.
terms.exampleUsage: हमें हमेशा संयम रखना चाहिए। (We should always maintain self-control.)

terms.definition: A lesson or moral - the teaching or message we learn from a story. Every good story has a seekh. The seekh of this story is that we should not be greedy and should not lose our sense just because something is free.
terms.exampleUsage: इस कहानी से बहुत अच्छी सीख मिलती है। (We get a very good lesson from this story.)

terms.definition: An idiom - a phrase or expression whose meaning is different from the literal meaning of its words. Hindi has many muhavare. For example, 'लालच बुरी बला' (greed is an evil thing) is related to this chapter's message. Muhavare make Hindi language rich and colourful.
terms.exampleUsage: 'लालच बुरी बला' एक प्रसिद्ध मुहावरा है। ('Greed is evil' is a famous idiom.)
